User.title: User
User.description: Represents an authenticated user of the AI Lo-Fi Channel Factory application. Users manage channels, projects, and content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

displayName.description: The user's display name or chosen username.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Channel.title: Channel
Channel.description: Represents a YouTube channel managed within the application, where Lo-Fi content is published.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Channel entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns or primarily manages this channel. (Relationship: User 1:N Channel)

name.description: The display name of the YouTube channel.

youtubeChannelId.description: The official YouTube channel ID, obtained after connecting to the YouTube Data API.

status.description: Current status of the channel within the app (e.g., 'Active', 'Pending Setup', 'Disconnected', 'Archived').

description.description: A description of the channel's content and niche.

brandingImageUrl.description: URL to the channel's branding image (e.g., banner or avatar).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the channel entity was created in the app.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the channel entity was last updated in the app.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a strategic content initiative or a 'Lo-Fi channel project' focusing on a specific niche or theme, managed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created or owns this project. (Relationship: User 1:N Project)

name.description: A descriptive name for the content project (e.g., 'Halloween Lo-Fi Vibes').

description.description: Detailed description of the project's goals, niche, and content strategy.

artistSuggestions.description: AI-suggested artist styles or specific artists relevant to the project's theme.

thematicConcepts.description: AI-suggested thematic concepts to explore within the project (e.g., 'Rainy Day', 'Cyberpunk City').

targetChannelIds.description: References to Channel entities that this project's content is intended for. (Relationship: Project N:N Channel)

contentParameters.description: Structured input or a JSON string containing parameters for content generation, potentially from spreadsheet uploads.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the project was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the project was last updated.

VideoIdea.title: VideoIdea
VideoIdea.description: Represents an initial concept or blueprint for a video to be produced, often derived from AI analysis or user input.

id.description: Unique identifier for the VideoIdea entity.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project under which this video idea was generated. (Relationship: Project 1:N VideoIdea)

titleSuggestion.description: An AI-generated or user-provided title suggestion for the video.

descriptionSuggestion.description: An AI-generated or user-provided description outline for the video.

keywordsSuggestion.description: AI-generated or user-provided SEO keywords for the video.

targetAudience.description: Description of the target audience for this video idea.

moodTone.description: The desired mood or tone of the video (e.g., 'Chill', 'Study', 'Relaxing').

durationMinutes.description: The intended duration of the video in minutes (e.g., 30 for long-form, 1 for shorts).

status.description: Current production status of the video idea (e.g., 'Pending AI Generation', 'Script Generated', 'Ready for Review', 'Content Generated', 'Completed').

scriptId.description: Reference to the Script entity generated for this video idea. (Relationship: VideoIdea 1:1 Script)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the video idea was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the video idea was last updated.

Script.title: Script
Script.description: Stores the textual content of a script generated for a video, often by an AI agent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Script entity.

videoIdeaId.description: Reference to the VideoIdea this script belongs to. (Relationship: VideoIdea 1:1 Script)

content.description: The full text content of the generated script.

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if the script was generated by an AI.

aiPrompt.description: The prompt used to instruct the AI to generate this script.

reviewStatus.description: Status of the script review (e.g., 'Pending Review', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'Revised').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the script was generated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the script was last updated or reviewed.

MediaAsset.title: MediaAsset
MediaAsset.description: Represents any media file (audio, image, video segment) generated or used in the content production pipeline, stored in Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MediaAsset entity.

videoIdeaId.description: Reference to the VideoIdea this asset is associated with. (Relationship: VideoIdea 1:N MediaAsset)

storagePath.description: The full path to the media file within Firebase Storage.

fileName.description: The original file name of the media asset.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the stored media file (e.g., 'audio/mp3', 'image/png', 'video/mp4').

description.description: A brief description of what this media asset represents (e.g., 'AI-generated chill beat', 'Lo-Fi cafe animation').

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if this media asset was generated by an AI tool.

aiPrompt.description: The prompt used to instruct the AI for generating this asset, if applicable.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the media asset in seconds, if it's audio or video.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the media asset entity was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the media asset entity was last updated.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a fully assembled video artifact ready for YouTube publishing, combining generated audio and visuals.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

videoIdeaId.description: Reference to the VideoIdea that led to the creation of this video. (Relationship: VideoIdea 1:1 Video)

title.description: The final, SEO-optimized title for the YouTube video.

description.description: The comprehensive, SEO-optimized description for the YouTube video.

tags.description: A strategic set of tags and hashtags for YouTube SEO.

thumbnailAssetId.description: Reference to the MediaAsset entity representing the video's thumbnail. (Relationship: Video 1:1 MediaAsset)

videoAssetId.description: Reference to the MediaAsset entity representing the final compiled video file. (Relationship: Video 1:1 MediaAsset)

status.description: Current status of the video (e.g., 'Assembling', 'Rendering', 'Ready for Review', 'Approved', 'Scheduled', 'Uploaded', 'Failed').

uploadTargetChannelId.description: Reference to the Channel entity where this video is intended to be uploaded. (Relationship: Channel 1:N Video)

scheduledUploadDate.description: The planned date and time for the video to be uploaded to YouTube.

actualUploadDate.description: The actual date and time the video was uploaded to YouTube.

youtubeVideoId.description: The unique ID assigned by YouTube after a successful upload.

isShort.description: Indicates if the video is formatted as a YouTube Short.

isLiveStream.description: Indicates if this video is a component for a persistent 24/7 live stream.

beatMonetizationUrl.description: URL to an external beat-selling platform for monetizing the audio track.

licensingInfo.description: Details regarding the licensing of music or visual assets used in the video.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the video entity was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the video entity was last updated (e.g., status change, metadata update).

Analytics.title: Analytics
Analytics.description: Stores performance metrics and sales data for channels and videos, collected from YouTube and external platforms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Analytics entry.

videoId.description: Reference to the Video entity this analytic data pertains to. (Relationship: Video 1:N Analytics)

channelId.description: Reference to the Channel entity this analytic data pertains to (for channel-level metrics). (Relationship: Channel 1:N Analytics)

eventType.description: The type of analytic event or metric (e.g., 'Views', 'CTR', 'WatchTime', 'BeatSale').

value.description: The numeric value of the recorded metric.

date.description: The date for which this analytic data was recorded.

source.description: The source platform from which the data was collected (e.g., 'YouTube', 'BeatStars', 'Gumroad').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the analytic entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the analytic entry was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) corresponds to `request.auth.uid` and serves as the owner identifier for this document.
Represents YouTube channels managed by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document (as per schema) acts as the ownerId for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this channel.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Channel document.

definition.description: Stores strategic content projects owned by a specific user. The 'userId' field in the document (as per schema) acts as the ownerId for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Project document.

definition.description: Represents conceptual video ideas generated within a project. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (holding the `userId` from the parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Project.

params.name: videoIdeaId
params.description: The unique identifier for the VideoIdea document.

definition.description: Stores the textual content of scripts for a specific video idea. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (holding the `userId` from the parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Project.

params.name: videoIdeaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent VideoIdea.

params.name: scriptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Script document.

definition.description: Stores metadata for media files (audio, image, video segments) used in a video idea. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (holding the `userId` from the parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Project.

params.name: videoIdeaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent VideoIdea.

params.name: mediaAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the MediaAsset document.

definition.description: Represents a fully assembled video artifact ready for YouTube publishing, linked to a video idea. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (holding the `userId` from the parent path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns the parent project.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Project.

params.name: videoIdeaId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent VideoIdea.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Video document.

definition.description: Stores performance metrics for videos and channels. Each document includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field (the User's UID) for direct ownership authorization and secure list queries (QAPs).

params.name: analyticsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Analytics entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a hierarchical, user-centric model for user-specific content and a top-level collection with denormalized ownership for aggregate data, prioritizing Authorization Independence and QAPs.  

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**  
Authorization independence is achieved by denormalizing the `ownerId` (the user's unique ID, `request.auth.uid`) into every document that belongs to a user's workflow or is part of a subcollection that needs granular, atomic authorization.  
*   For `User`, `Channel`, and `Project` documents, the `userId` field (present in their respective schemas and part of the document's path for `Channel` and `Project`) explicitly denotes ownership. Security rules for these entities can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `userId` in the path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...` implies `request.auth.uid == userId`) or with the `resource.data.userId` field for `Channel` and `Project`.  
*   For nested entities like `VideoIdea`, `Script`, `MediaAsset`, and `Video`, which are descendants of a `Project` (and thus indirectly a `User`), an explicit `ownerId` field (holding the `userId` of the project's owner) is denormalized into each document. This eliminates the need for expensive and insecure `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules (e.g., `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(userId)/projects/$(projectId)).data.ownerId`). Instead, rules can simply use `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId;`. This strategy ensures atomic operations (transactions, batch writes) are secure and straightforward, as all authorization context is self-contained within the document being accessed.  
*   The `Analytics` collection, being a top-level collection, also requires an explicit `ownerId` field within each document. This allows a user to access their analytics without relying on any parent document's context.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**  
The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations (QAPs) through consistent ownership modeling:
*   **Hierarchical User-Owned Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/channels`, `/users/{userId}/projects`, and their nested subcollections (`videoIdeas`, `scripts`, `mediaAssets`, `videos`) leverage the `userId` in the path and/or a denormalized `ownerId` in the document. A user can only `list` documents within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/...` hierarchy, which is inherently secure. The security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` wildcard in the path, ensuring that `list` requests automatically filter to only owned resources.  
*   **Top-Level Analytics with Denormalized Owner:** For the `/analytics` collection, `list` queries will be secured by requiring `request.auth.uid` to match a query filter on the denormalized `ownerId` field (e.g., `db.collection('analytics').where('ownerId', '==', currentUser.uid).get()`). The Firestore security rule for `list` on `/analytics` would enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == request.query.where.ownerId;`, preventing users from listing all analytics entries or those belonging to others.  

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**  
*   All documents within a given collection or subcollection (e.g., `/users/{userId}/channels`) share the same security posture (owned by `userId`). This simplifies rules by avoiding mixed access requirements within the same collection.  
*   Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) is consistently applied for all user-specific content, making ownership explicit and rules easy to write.  

This robust design ensures maintainable, scalable, and secure Firestore interactions while adhering to all core principles and mandates.
